name: Santa Fe Spicy Baked Black Beans (Mandel)
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon pureed chipotles chiles, in adobo sauce
OR double that amount -- to taste
2 dried ancho chiles, softened in hot water -- drained and stemmed
45 ounces cooked black beans, rinsed and drained (low-sodium preferred)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat oil in a 1-quart saucepan over medium-high heat.
2. When hot, add onion and stir-fry until softened, about 4 minutes.
3. Add the chipotle puree, catsup, tomato juice, brown sugar, 1 diced tomato, cider vinegar, red wine vinegar, molasses, ancho chiles, salt to taste, and 1/2 cup water.
4. Stir well. Taste and add more chipotle puree if desired.
5. Simmer uncovered for 10 minutes.
6. Puree in a food processor.
7. Pour into a 2-quart casserole and stir in beans.
8. Cover and bake at 350°F (175°C) for 1 hour.
9. Taste and add salt if needed.
10. Add up to 1/2 cup more water if the mixture is too thick.
11. Serve hot in warm corn tortillas if desired, garnished with remaining diced tomato, cilantro, green onions, and sour cream if desired.
[10 servings. Each of 10 servings without tortilla or garnishes: 185 calories; 205 mg sodium; 0 cholesterol; 2 grams fat; 27 grams carbohydrates; 12 grams protein; 6.32 grams fiber.]
Pinto beans or black-eyed peas may be substituted for some of the black beans to add variety. The casserole is easily doubled or tripled.
